data.content:
The file is generated successfully but never added to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild globs expand at **evaluation time** (before any targets run), so the generated file is invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `<Compile>` and `<FileWrites>` inside the target:

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and track it for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild globs are evaluated at evaluation time (before targets run), so the generated file isn't picked up automatically.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group within the target, and also register it in `<FileWrites>` so it gets cleaned properly.

Let me use the `including-generated-files` skill since it's specifically about this problem.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and track it for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Root cause:** MSBuild implicit globs (`**/*.cs`) expand during **evaluation** — before any targets execute. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the file at **execution** time, so the compiler never sees it.

**Fix applied:** Added an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target to:
1. **`<Compile Include="...">`** — explicitly add the generated file to compilation
2. **`<FileWrites Include="...">`** — register it so `dotnet clean` removes it
